content.campus_location:
Google Maps

Campus Map

Virtual Campus Tour

📔 Module(Course) Catalogue

Course Selection

Before starting the application process, you are asked to make a wish list of your desired courses. You will need to check if you meet the entry and language requirements, as well as the specific criteria per faculty. Your course wish list represents your intended course preferences or field of study, but please note that this is not a definitive list, as it is still dependent on availability and on possible changes in the course schedule.

You will have to take the majority of your courses at your host faculty (the faculty that you were nominated for). In some cases it is possible to take one or maximum two courses in another faculty.* This can only be arranged on a case-by-case basis by the international officer of your accepting faculty, and is dependent on availability of courses and possible course requirements. We advise you to select courses that represent your current study level and study background.

As a Bachelor's (undergraduate) student you are registered on a non-degree basis and you should select Bachelor's courses. Access to Master's (graduate) courses is very limited and restricted. Admission to courses at Master’s level is awarded on a case-by-case basis and only if you already have a Bachelor’s degree in the desired field of study. As a Master's student you will only be able to follow courses for a maximum of one semester (full year is not allowed). Acceptance at a UvA faculty does not automatically make you eligible for all courses in that faculty, as some courses may have specific entry requirements or prerequisites. Also, keep in mind that the course schedule and availability of courses are subject to change.

If you are accepted by the Amsterdam University College (AUC) you can only register for AUC courses.

The official registration for your courses will only take place after acceptance. Due to possible changes in the course schedule, the UvA cannot guarantee that the courses of your wish list will actually be available during course registration. It is also possible that at the time of your application, the latest course schedule has not been released yet. For application purposes, you may therefore select courses from the most recent academic year available.

Global Exchange Programme for Bachelor’s Students

For courses in Economics and Business, students should have completed at least two full academic years of courses in one of the fields of study below. Students should also have passed courses in Mathematics and Statistics.

(General) Economics

Business Economics

Econometrics

Actuarial Sciences

Management

Marketing

For courses in Humanities, students should have taken courses in any of the following subject areas for at least one full academic year. Please note that we require a theoretical academic background in at least one of the fields of study below:

Art, Religion or Cultural Studies

Archeology and Ancient studies

History and American studies

Dutch Studies

European Studies

English Language and Literature

Literature, Language and Culture studies

Media studies

Philosophy

For courses in Science, students should fulfill the prerequisites as follows:

Artificial Intelligence - application possible for 3rd year students of AI (and related sciences), English taught courses in semester 1 only.

Biology - application possible for 3rd year students of Biology, most English-taught courses are offered in semester 1.

Biomedical Sciences - application possible for 3rd year students of Biomedical Sciences (and related sciences), English taught courses in semester 1 only.

Chemistry - application possible for 3rd year students of Chemistry.

Future Planet Studies - application possible for 2nd and 3rd year students of Environmental Studies (and related sciences).

Mathematics - application possible for 3rd year students of Mathematics.

Physics and Astronomy - application possible for 3rd year students of Physics and Astronomy, most English-taught courses are offered in semester 1.

Psychobiology - application possible for 3rd year students with a Biology or Psychology background, English-taught courses in semester 1 only.

If there is not a full semester load of courses offered in your own field of study, you can choose additional Interdisciplinary courses or courses in Scientific Programming, both at Bachelor's level (see below):

Interdisciplinary courses – application possible for students of all study areas

Courses in Scientific Programming – application possibly for students with a Science background

The Law programme is intended for advanced law students who have completed at least two full academic years in law courses. The law programme is open for bachelor students, but most law courses are offered at master’s level. Law students can apply for:

European Private Law courses

European Union Law courses

European Union Competition Law and Regulation courses

International Trade and Investment Law courses

Public International Law courses

The College of Politics, Psychology, Law and Economics (PPLE): Today’s major global issues, such as the rise of populism, the debate between security and privacy, or global trade conflicts, are best addressed by a combined analysis through the lens of Politics, Psychology, Law and Economics. Incoming exchange students are eligible to take PPLE major courses offered in years 2 and 3, interdisciplinary electives and interdisciplinary seminars. Due to the small-scale nature of the PPLE programme and the intense workload of an honours programme, PPLE college reserves the right to review applications on motivation, academic background, study progress and GPA. Incoming exchange students must have previously taken at least 30 ECTS (or equivalent) of courses in at last two of the four PPLE disciplines.

For courses in Social and Behavioural Sciences, students should have taken courses in any of the subject areas below for at least one full academic year in order to be able to follow second-year courses. It is recommended to have accomplished courses on statistics previously. Note that in order to have access to specialisation modules, a background of two years in a specific field is required.

Sociology

Cultural Anthropology

Communication Science

Human Geography

Urban planning

Political Science

Interdisciplinary Social Sciences

Child Development and Education

Psychology

Amsterdam University College is UvA’s honours college that offers a small-scale and intensive, international Liberal Arts and Sciences programme, taught entirely in English. A key aspect of this interdisciplinary programme is tackling real-life problems and challenges facing today’s world. All students live and study together on the AUC international campus in Amsterdam East. Exchange students can apply to study at AUC with one and a half years of academic experience, but they must have a high GPA and a strong motivation. Due to the small-scale nature and space constraints in their own dormitories, Amsterdam University College has relatively limited availability and therefore reserves the right to review applications on motivation, academic background, study progress and GPA.

content.accommodation:
General info:

Here are some tips on avoiding scams in the Dutch rental market:

Does the offer sound too good to be true? Then it probably is.

Always check the address of a room that is on offer; e.g. via Google, Google Maps.

Always check the credentials of the person offering a room; e.g. ask the person to provide a telephone number. Landline phone numbers in Maastricht start with 043 or +31-43 when the country code of the Netherlands is added. Google whether a name has been used before in a fraud scheme. Ask if you can register with the council at the address. If not, that's a red flag.

Never trust people who claim not to be able to arrange a viewing; e.g. because they are not in the Netherlands.

Never transfer money in advance

Never transfer money to Western Union, PayPal accounts or to foreign accounts.

content.about_text:
Housing issue:

The demand on housing in many Dutch university cities is tough, and Tilburg is no exception. A room in our reserved accommodation cannot be guaranteed for all students, meaning many students will need to search on the private market for a room. Students are advised to start their search early, and not to travel to Tilburg until they have secured a room.

We welcome students of all backgrounds to Tilburg and aim to support students with additional needs. We are live on the Inclusive Mobility EU website, where you can read more about our services for your students.

For spring semester: Securing housing could indeed be challenging. As I am sure you know, there is a housing shortage in the Netherlands and the search for housing is particularly tough in the Fall semesters, as many local/full degree students (as well as the exchange students) are also searching for housing around July/August. Our dedicated housing office (studenthousing@tilburguniversity.edu) is there to offer advice and, if needed, emergency accommodation. Your students might have an advantage, for example if they know a friend/fellow exchange student who is leaving Tilburg after their first semester and they are able to take over their room/apartment. We also have a sub-letting scheme whereby our outgoing students can rent out their room to an international student whilst on exchange, but overall the housing situation remains tough due to high demand and lower supply. It would be good for your students to be able to consider the pros and cons.

Search through over 250 courses in English for exchange students. Select a faculty and open an overview of the courses offered and the faculty contact information.

Art History

French Language and Culture (courses offered in French)

German Language and Culture (courses offered in German)

History

Linguistics

North American Studies

Spanish Language and Culture (courses offered in Spanish)

The Faculty of Law offers exchange courses in Law and European Law.

The minimum workload for exchange students in Law is 15 ECTS per semester. We advise students to not aim at obtaining over 30 ECTS, because all our courses require serious investment. On average, students pass between 3 and 5 courses. The number of courses you choose also depends on the conditions set by your home university.

Before choosing a course and including it to your Learning Agreement, please read the course description carefully and make sure that you meet the prerequisites.

To qualify as an advanced exchange student, students must be in the final year of their bachelor’s or in their master’s programme in Law and have at least earned 160 ECTS in law courses.

As an exchange student of the Law faculty, you may also take a maximum of two courses at other faculties (in consultation). Please find the complete Radboud University Exchange Student Course Offer here.

The Faculty of Medical Sciences offers exchange courses in (Bio-)Medicine and Dentistry.

Biosciences

Molecular Sciences

Anthropology and Development Studies (Bachelor's and Master's)

Educational Science (Master's)

Sociology (Bachelor's and Master's)

Gender and Diversity (Bachelor's)

Bachelor students are not allowed to take courses at master level. A Bachelor’s diploma is one of the mandatory prerequisites for our master courses.

All English-taught courses of the Faculty of Philosophy, Theology and Religious Studies can be followed by exchange students. As an exchange student in PTRS you are also allowed to take courses in other faculties of Radboud University.

The Dutch grading system is an absolute grading system, awarded on a scale from 0 to 10

Grades from 6 upwards are regarded as 'pass'

In order to be awarded a diploma, all grades must be at least 6

universities.english_notes:
<Language Requirements>
• Our educational system - Problem Based Learning (PBL) - requires you to actively take part in the tutorial meetings. The PBL method is explicitly student-centred rather than teacher-centred, and the students are expected to take the initiative. Speaking up and actively taking part in discussions is a crucial part of the system and this will also influence your final grades. Therefore, it is extremely important that you have an excellent knowledge of both written and spoken English.
universities.available_areas:
Students can apply for Business and Economics major only.
https://www.maastrichtuniversity.nl/education/exchange/sbe#incoming
universities.restricted_areas: Only students majoring in Business and Economics can apply.
universities.admission_notes:
<Semester Fulfillment Requirement>
• Bachelor's student have to have completed at least two semesters of relevant study before they start their exchange programme at SBE. This entails successfully finishing at least 10 courses related to their major.

<Course Choice / Field of Study>
• Only the Students majoring/minoring in Economics or Business can apply. (For majors less related to business/ economics such as Culture and Design Management, please contact Yonsei OIA first before applying)
• Master’s level students have to have a valid and relevant bachelor’s degree when they start their exchange. Students who don't have enough academic background in Economics & Business can be refused to be admitted.
• If you want to take master’s level courses while at SBE, you have to have a valid and relevant bachelor’s degree/diploma.

Problem-Based Learning (PBL)

Conversion of grades and credits

Grades are rounded to one decimal, on a scale from 0.0 to 10.0 (a 10.0 is very rarely scored)

A grade of 5.5 is considered a pass; anything lower is a fail.

SBE will send an electronic version of your grade transcript directly to you and your home university.

If you have spent the fall semester at SBE, you will receive your grade transcript in January / February. If you have spent the spring semester or entire academic year at SBE, you will receive your grade transcript in July / August.

✏️ Academic Requirements

📔 Module(Course) Catalogue

New Course Registration Process:

While in the past ‘we’ assigned students to their preferred courses, students now self-register for courses via a digital platform on a first-come, first-served basis. The courses we offered to our incoming exchange students in the year 2024-2025 are available on this page under course offer. The course offers for 2025-2026 will be updated in June. Please keep in mind that some courses have limited capacity and are very popular among exchange students. So, it is important that students do their course registration on time and are flexible with their choices and have sufficient back-up courses during the course registration.
content.accommodation:
📱 Social media

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/ErasmusUniversity

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/erasmusuniversity/

Twitter: https://x.com/erasmusuni

Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@ErasmusUniversity

📅 Academic Calendar

Concluding exchange before end of January

Please note that the ESHCC academic calendar for the Fall semester ends around 31 January. Most courses at ESHCC include mandatory attendance and/or examinations, meaning students must stay until the end of January to successfully complete these courses.

The only exception is the mandatory course - ‘Feeling Orange’ (5 EC)- which concludes a week after the Christmas break, requires no mandatory attendance, and involves only an online assignment. Unfortunately, we cannot offer other alternatives at ESHCC for students who leave early e.g. Online exams or exemptions to mandatory attendance.

Could you please inform us if your students need to return home before January and specify the number of ECs they need to fulfill during their exchange? This will help us discuss together the best course of action.

content.about_text:
📁 Factsheet and documents

https://www.eur.nl/en/media/2024-08-ba-exchange-offer-2024-20250

Established in 1913, Erasmus School of Economics has an outstanding reputation for academic excellence and offers top-level programmes in all major areas of economics and business. In line with its founding goal of making a contribution to society, Erasmus School of Economics has always put the transfer of knowledge to the wider community at the core of its mission.

From its location in the heart of the European economy – Rotterdam – the School links communities all around the world, from advisory work with Dutch government to research partnerships with other top institutions and companies.

Erasmus School of Economics is part of the Erasmus University Rotterdam.

“Most of our courses require at least a basic knowledge of maths or statistics, and we have noticed that when we have approved students without this knowledge in the past, they have failed all courses here and simply not had an enjoyable exchange experience. Also, many of our courses consist out of group assignments, and it’s not fair for the other students in the group if one student cannot follow the assignment properly.”

Strategy Flower 2022-2027

content.about_text:
You want to spend one or two semester at one of the most beautiful harbor cities in Europe? Then come to Universität Hamburg. It is located right in the heart of the city and belongs with 42.000 students to one of the 10 biggest universities in Germany. Among our 170+ degree programs you will find a program that fits best to your major at home.

For information about Universität Hamburg please visit our website or our Instagram profile.

In case you have any questions, do not hesitate to contact us via email: Zentralaustausch@uni-hamburg.de

A detailed course catalog (kommentiertes Vorlesungsverzeichnis) for each subject is available online in the study information net “STiNE”.
www.stine.uni-hamburg.de ➔ Vorlesungsverzeichnis

The Faculties of Business Administration and Business, Economics and Social Sciences only accept students enrolled in degree programs corresponding to these faculties (e.g., students of German studies will not be admitted).

English taught courses offered by the Faculty of Business, Economics and Social Sciences: STiNE > Course Catalogue > Overview > Faculty Business, Economics and Social Sciences > Course Program for International Incoming Students

English taught courses offered by the Faculty of Law: STiNE> Course Catalogue > Overview > Faculty of Law > International Exchange
Students > English taught courses

English taught courses offered by the Faculty of Business Administration:
STiNE > Course Catalogue > Overview > Faculty of Business Administration> International Incoming Students > Bachelor/Master
A limited number of courses on the graduate level are taught in English.

Please note: The list of English language master programs of Universität Hamburg provides an overview on subjects taught in English, but not all programs accept exchange students. If graduate students plan to take courses in English, please contact UHH first.

A current list of master programs offered in English can be found on the UHH Website.

Faculty of Mathematics, Informatics and Natural Sciences is prepared to hold classes in English if international students participate. There are other fields of studies such as Political Sciences, Economics, Philosophy, English and American Studies, Asia Africa Studies, Anthropology etc.
https://www.uni-hamburg.de/en/campuscenter/studienangebot.html#Abschluss=opt3&Fakultaet=&sort=&index=

Incoming exchange students can take German language courses free of charge if space is available. Pre-semester intensive language courses (usually A1-B1) start approx. two weeks prior to the start of the semester.

All students of Universität Hamburg, including exchange students, pay a mandatory contribution fee per semester (reduced fee for exchange
students in winter semester 2020/21: approx. EUR 278), which includes a public transport pass for the Hamburg Public Transport Area.
Exchange info: www.uni-hamburg.de/en-central-exchanges-incoming
Image film of the university: https://lecture2go.uni-hamburg.de/l2go/-/get/v/36994
Brochure “To-Dos“: www.uni-hamburg.de/piasta/welcome.pdf
Events/seminars/advising: www.uni-hamburg.de/piasta_e.html
Buddy programs: www.uni-hamburg.de/piasta/welcome-buddy_e.html

content.about_text:
📁 Factsheet and documents

Presentation about Pforzheim University (specially for Incoming students)

Pforzheim University

Pforzheim University with its three schools - the School of Design, the School of Engineering and the Business School - enjoys a first-class reputation. The schools combine creativity with business education and technical precision. This combination also makes the university an attractive science and research partner for the regional and national economy. With around 6,200 students, Pforzheim University - founded in 1877 - is one of the largest universities of applied sciences in the state of Baden-Württemberg.

The qualifications and practical experience of the teaching staff on the one hand and intensive cooperation with successful companies on the other lay the foundation for the university's outstanding position in the rankings. Pforzheim University offers 28 bachelor's and 19 master's degree programs in the schools of Design, Engineering, and Business and Law. It enjoys a high level of acceptance in research and teaching, which also results from the good supervision ratio. Around 480 professors and lecturers ensure that students feel they are in good hands in Pforzheim right from the start.

The close connection between theory and practice develops positive synergy effects that result in numerous projects and research approaches for industry and business. The Institute for Applied Research, numerous Steinbeis Transfer Centers or the Jewelry Technology Institute are just a few successful examples of this.

The university cooperates with more than 100 partner universities worldwide, is involved in international networks and attaches great importance to business and corporate ethics. The university was one of the first in the world to commit to the Principles for Responsible Management Education (PRME) initiated by the UN.

Leading through a change of perspective

What is the vision that drives us? What is our mission statement and how do we realize our goals for our students, our partners in business and society in the future? What self-image do we live? On our German pages you will find answers to these questions and all important information about the new strategy of Pforzheim University.

Master’s degree programs
PhD program
Special classes for exchange students
Modules
Study & Exam regulations
Course catalogue
Course Registration
Enrollment in the following courses is subject to availability of lab classes and to prior approval by the institute/department: biochemistry, bioinformatics, and biology. Restrictions apply in pharmacy, law, business administration, and economics.
The Law Department prefers direct exchange students from institutions who also accept law students. There are only very few places available for students from other exchange programs.
The School of Business and Economics requires excellent German language skills (B2 level or above) for classes taught in German. There is a very limited number of classes in English, and they are not always offered regularly.
Please note that Human Medicine and Veterinary Medicine are NOT open to exchange students.
Freie Universität Berlin offers a range number of master’s degree programs. The following programs are open for postgraduate exchange students without restriction: modern German literature, philosophy, and political science.
All other master’s degree programs are restricted and need prior approval from the institute/department.

content.about_text:
📁 Factsheet and documents

Study at FUB

Life in Berlin

Situated in the southwestern part of Berlin, in the district of Düppel.

Main campus: Campus, which is home to most of the university’s facilities.

Founded in 1948 in West Berlin.

Established as a response to the political pressures on universities in East Germany.

Public research university.

Known for its emphasis on research and interdisciplinary studies.

Composed of 12 departments and several interdisciplinary research centers.

Offers a broad range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs.

Primarily German, though many graduate programs and courses are available in English, especially in fields like business, economics, and some sciences.

Highly regarded for its research output and academic excellence.

Frequently ranked among the top universities in Germany and Europe.

Noted for strong research in the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences.

Includes prominent scholars, researchers, and public figures such as philosopher Jürgen Habermas and historian Martin Sabine.

Active in numerous international partnerships and exchange programs.

Engages in global research collaborations and has numerous international student exchanges.

Features modern campus buildings, libraries, and research centers.

The campus is designed to foster an open and collaborative academic environment.

Home to a diverse student body with various cultural and academic backgrounds.

Offers numerous student organizations, clubs, and activities.

Hosts various cultural events, lectures, and public discussions.

Strong emphasis on public engagement and fostering community relationships.

Continues to adapt and expand its research facilities and academic offerings.

Focuses on sustainability and innovation in education and research.

Campus Map

📔 Module(Course) Catalogue

All courses take place once per week (90 minutes, or equivalent online), with up to 23 students per course. At the end of the semester, there is a written exam. Students may obtain 5 ECTS credits per course.

As from the winter semester 22/23, all courses are open to students from all academic fields. 50% of the courses are taught in German (at B1 level), others in English (B2 level). The courses are designed for undergraduate students in their second/ third year. Postgraduate students are welcome, but are advised to check with their home university whether they will get credits for these courses.

✏Courses for international exchange students✏
content.accommodation:
The Accommodation Service arranges accommodation for admitted international exchange and program students of Freie Universität Berlin.

Housing Options : A certain contingent of accommodations is available in the privately operated Studentendorf Schlachtensee as well as rooms in student dormitories of the Studierendenwerk. In addition, the Accommodation Service arranges furnished single apartments, studio apartments, and single rooms with shared kitchens.

Registration : Registration is usually possible from June for the winter semester and from December at the earliest for the summer semester.

Criteria : The number of places is limited and students are allocated on a "first come, first served" basis.

content.student_tips:
#여행에는용이X #조용한 #흐린날씨

지리적 위치 : 유럽 여행을 하기에 베를린은 썩 좋은 위치가 아님. 따라서 학기 중에는 대부분 독일 내에서만 여행을 다니고, 학기가 끝난 후에 본격적으로 여행을 다님.

기후 : 2월~8월까지 머물렀는데, 한국의 한겨울 한여름만큼 춥거나 더웠던 날은 거의 없었음. 4월까지 꽤 쌀쌀한 날씨가 이어짐. 여름에는 9시나 10시가 지나서 해가 짐. 유럽은 에어컨이 없는 곳이 많다 보니, 더위 때문에 힘들 때도 있었는데, 비 오고 난 뒤면 1~2주 정도 선선해서 견딜만 했음. 습도가 낮아 그늘에만 가도 훨씬 시원. 햇빛은 엄청 따가움.

출처: “베를린에서 한학기 (2022 봄)”

지리적 위치 : 시내 중심이 아니라 조용한 외곽에 떨어져 있음. 베를린이 독일 다른 도시에 비해 여행다니기 아주 좋은 위치는 아님. 하지만 동유럽 버스 여행이 가능하고 신공항이 있어 나쁘지 않은 것 같음.

기후 : 2월 말에 도착하여 8월 말에 한국으로 돌아옴. 베를린 날씨는 변덕스럽고 우중충함. 1학기 파견이라 여름에는 날씨가 좋은 기간이 길어 좋았음. 에어컨이 없으나, 버틸 수 있을 정도.

출처: “팬데믹 속 교환학생 (2021 봄)”

지리적 위치 : 다른 독일의 대도시들과 조금 거리가 있고, 유럽의 도시를 놀러다니기에도 애매한 위치에 있음.

기후 : 겨울에는 그렇게 좋지 못함. 비가 자주 오고, 푸른 하늘을 잘 볼 수 없음.

출처: “베를린에서의 1학기 (2021 가을)”

#잔잔함 #부촌 #안전함

학교 주변은 잔잔하고, 식당이나 놀거리가 많지는 않음. 학교가 워낙 베를린 구석에 있어서, 공강을 만드는 것은 추천하지 않음.

출처: “베를린에서 한학기 (2022 봄)”

베를린의 풍경은 완전 유럽스럽다기보단 어딘가 폐허같기도 하면서 예술적이고 힙함. 다양한 인종, 국적의 사람들이 많이 살고 있고 웬만하면 다 영어로 소통이 될 정도로 국제적인 도시임.

출처: “팬데믹 속 교환학생 (2021 봄)”

상당히 조용하고 상권도 많이 발달되지 않은 곳. 독일의 부촌에 해당하는 도시라고 함.

건물이 깨끗하고 안전함.

출처: “베를린에서의 1학기 (2021 가을)”

#기숙사 #한식많음 #마트물가저렴

사설 기숙사 6~7곳에 컨택을 했는데, 결과적으로 방이 비어있던 곳은 단 한 곳 뿐. 그곳과 계약했고, 침실은 혼자, 부엌과 화장실은 이웃 1명과 공유하는 형태였음.

저는 도착하자마자 25유로 짜리 OK 밥솥을 사서 매일 밥을 먹었어요. 베를린에는 아시안 마트가 많고, 온갖 종류의 조미료와 한식 재료들을 팖.

점심은 주로 학교 식당에서 먹음. 솔직히 음식이 맛있진 않지만, 3~6유로 정도로 값이 싸 애용.

자유대 역에는 컵밥과 치킨을 파는 한식당 키오스크가 있어요. 가격이 싸진 않지만, 주인분들이 친절하심.

출처: “베를린에서 한학기 (2022 봄)”

성별은 웬만하면 맞춰 주지만, 아닌 경우도 있음.

방이 큰 편이고, 한국 기숙사같은 복도형임.

단점은 시내에서 거리가 있음. 또한 부엌을 쓰는 인원이 비교적 많음. (8명) 부엌을 더럽게 쓰는 친구들이 많아서 불편했음.

아무나 복도에 들어올 수 있다 보니 친구들을 데려와 시끄럽게 파티를 한다거나 외부인이 출입하는 경우가 있음.

시설이 낡은 편.

주변에 도보 5~10분 거리 슈퍼마켓이 3개 있음. 아시안마트는 대중교통으로 30분 정도 나가야 있음.

배달앱 주문 불가 지역임.

독일 외식 물가는 비싼 편. 마트 물가는 한국보다 쌈.

베를린에 아시아 식당 굉장히 많음.

초반에 아시안마트에 가서 조미료를 구비해 놓는 것을 추천.

식기는 이케아에서 구매하는 것을 추천.

출처: “팬데믹 속 교환학생 (2021 봄)”

필자는 시내 중심에 있고 싶어서 Neon Wood 라는 학생 호텔에서 지냄. 한달 월세는 7-800유로 정도. 알렉산더 플라츠와 다양한 브런치 카페, 편집샵이 주변에 있고 시설이 정말 깨끗하고 안전했음.

1층 로비에서 다른 친구들과 네트워킹을 할 수 있는 행사도 진행했음.

배를린 중앙역에서 가깝기 때문에 기차를 타고 곳곳을 여행하기에 최고의 위치였음.

학교 통학은 1시간 이상이 소요되며, 같은 학교를 다니는 친구들이 없다면 외로울 수 있음.

출처: “베를린에서의 1학기 (2021 가을)”

#독일어수업 #세미나 #중심도서관

교환학생용 강의 - 각 5 ECTS : 2개는 영어 강의(Reading the City, Creative Writing), 2개는 독일어 강의(Sprecherziehung, Goldene Zwanziger in Berlin ) 교환학생 수업이다 보니 교수님께서 쉬운 단어로 천천히 설명해주시려고 노력하심.

도서관 : 인문 도서관(뇌 도서관)은 메인 건물에 있는 가장 큰 도서관. 들어갈 때 소지품을 모두 투명한 가방에 옮겨 담고 나머지는 사물함에 보관해야 함. 법학 도서관은 메인 건물이 아니라, 15분 정도 떨어진 곳에 위치한 건물이었는데, 이곳 역시 시설이 좋고 깨끗.

출처: “베를린에서 한학기 (2022 봄)”

교환학생용 수업은 대부분 세미나 형식. 들었던 수업 중 2개는 일주일에 2시간 실시간 화상 수업이었고, 미리 자료를 읽어 와서 오픈된 토론을 하는 방식이었음. 다른 수업 1개는 실시간 강의 없이 교수님이 정해주시는 주제에 대한 편지형 쪽글을 써서 제출하는 방식이었음.

독일어 수업은 일주일에 6시간 실시간 화상 강의로 진행. 레벨에 따라 수업 시간과 그에 따른 크레딧 차이가 있음.

출처: “팬데믹 속 교환학생 (2021 봄)”

필자는 수업 하나 제외 5개의 수업을 독일어로 들었으며, 어학코스는 레벨이 세분화되어 있지만 독일 역사 및 문화 수업들은 꽤 높은 독일어를 요구함.

Berliner Geschichte ergehen이라는 수업에서 다양한 공연도 보러가고, 뮤지엄도 공짜로 다니고, 장벽 앞에서 직접 보면서 역사 수업을 듣는 등 재미있던 기억이 있고, Sprecherziehung이라는 수업은 리딩 자료를 읽으며 발음 교정 수업인데 별다른 어려운 과제나 발표가 없던 수업이라 교환학생에서 1학기 채우기에 괜찮았던 수업. Wie tickt Deutschland라는 수업은 독일의 전반적인 경제 / 문화를 다양한 뮤지엄과 장소를 방문하며 배운 수업이라 만족도는 높지만, 독일어를 높이 요구하며 쪽지시험이나 독일어 PPT 발표 등이 있어 약간의 워크로드가 있었던 수업.

출처: “베를린에서의 1학기 (2021 가을)”

#언어교류 #탄뎀파트너 #자유로운도시

Uni Sport : 축구, 클라이밍, 요가, 보드, 태권도 등 종류가 다양. 유료이지만 비싸진 않았던 것 같음. 학기 초에 따로 신청을 해야 참여할 수 있음.

출처: “베를린에서 한학기 (2022 봄)”

굉장히 자유로운 도시임. 옷차림이 아주 자유롭고 다양함.

인종차별이 있었음.

페이스북 그룹 독일 유학생 네트워크에 올라오는 정보의 도움을 자주 받았음.

학생증이 있다면 베를린 내에서의 모든 대중교통은 구역에 상관없이 무료.

출처: “팬데믹 속 교환학생 (2021 봄)”

아날로그적인 도시. 카드가 안되는 경우가 여러 번 있었음.

출처: “베를린에서의 1학기 (2021 가을)”

content.about_text:
📁 Factsheet and documents

🌐 Websites

Study Abroad Website

Founded in 1970, the Sorbonne Nouvelle, which emerged from the former Faculty of Arts of the Université de Paris, combines the heritage of the Sorbonne with a spirit of continual innovation.

Offering an extensive range of high-level multidisciplinary courses in the fields of Arts, Humanities, Languages, and Social Sciences, which benefit from the support of its 30 research units, the Sorbonne Nouvelle awards Bachelor’s, Master’s and Doctoral degrees that attract students and researchers from all over the world.

These qualifications provide a wide variety of career opportunities: in teaching and research, publishing, library science, the performing arts (theatre, cinema and television), communications and journalism, cultural and creative industries, translation and interpreting, and in European and international relations.

The Sorbonne Nouvelle is ranked 2nd in France in Communication and Media Studies as well as in Modern Languages (QS 2021).

Film & Audio-Visual Studies

Theatre Studies

Communication & Media Studies

Cultural Industry Studies

Language Sciences & Language Pedagogy

Languages & Literatures: Afrikaans, Arabic, Basque, Catalan, Chinese, Dutch, English, French, Finnish, Galician, German, Hungarian, Hebrew, Italian, Persian, Portuguese, Romanian, Sanskrit, Spanish, etc.

Comparative literature

Translation and Interpreting

European and International Studies, Latin American Studies

These qualifications provide an extensive range of employment opportunities in creative and cultural industries, communications, journalism, the performing arts, publishing and library science, teaching and research, translation and interpreting, and cooperation and development. The Sorbonne Nouvelle also provides an ideal platform for students interested in European and international careers.

content.about_text:
Due to systematic issue, Sorbonne University cannot issue a transcript showing the number of credits earned for each courses. Therefore, students must use the attached learning agreement form when registering/registered for courses, which shows and confirms the number of credits for each courses registered. For inquiries, please contact abroad@yonsei.ac.kr

I remind you that the minimum French language certificate level B2 is obligatory.

We accept, as a French certificate, the results of an official test (such as the DELF B2/DALF/Alliance Française...) or the certificate signed by a French teacher from the student's home university. student, which certifies that the student is able to follow a course and complete work/exams in French. This certificate should be supplemented by an OLS test.

Please also remind your students who apply for an exchange at our faculty that there are no courses for exchange students only.

Our incoming international students are in class with French students and are treated like any other student.

Most of our courses are taught in French. The only courses taught in another language are available in the language departments.

And even then, there must be lessons both in French and in the language taught.

To find out more, your students can contact the academic coordinator of the study departments that interest them (see attached file).

Since September 2019, only academic coordinators can sign learning agreements. Your students can send their L.A. to the academic coordinator of the main department they chose during the online application.

No application will be accepted without all the required documents:

1 - The completed application form

2 - Copy of the ID card or passport

3 - Student card from the home university or proof of enrollment

4 - Proof of health insurance

5 - Minimum B2 level French language certificate

6 - Color ID photo (JPEG format for the Sorbonne University student card)

7 - Academic transcripts

8 - Resume

9 - Statement of purpose (motivation letter)

📁 Factsheet and documents

Sorbonne University is,

a multidisciplinary, research-intensive, world-class university. Through the strength of its 55,000 students, 7,300 academic researchers and partner researchers, and 3,900 administrative and technical staff, Sorbonne University promotes diversity, creativity, innovation and openness to the world.

The Faculty of Arts & Humanities at Sorbonne University is today the largest and most complete faculty in France, in the field of arts, languages, literature, social sciences and humanities. Committed to the transmission of a humanist culture, its pedagogical excellence is based on the recognized quality of its research to offer the most complete and innovative teaching.

International outreach

As an internationally renowned institution, the Faculty of Arts and Humanities has developed numerous cultural, scientific and educational partnerships with foreign universities and takes part in various exchange programmes on every continent. The Faculty thus welcomes many foreign students each year, and provides many different French and foreign language certifications.

Sorbonne University supports students to find accommodation in Paris.

Sorbonne University provides accommodation platform on their own.

Accommodation is also provided based on social criteria. To obtain a CROUS accommodation, students can create a DSE before signing up to Sorbonne University.

Sorbonne University provides personalised housing assistance to students. For students to benifit from the APL, they must apply to the CAF.

content.about_text:
We are happy to let you know that Paris School of Business will move to a new campus in September 2025. Built between 1883 and 1934 and located on 30 rue Claude Bernard, in the latin quarter (5th district), the Delta Campus hosted students from the Agronomic Institute of Paris who then became the engineering school AgroParisTech.

From its reopening after an ambitious renovation, the campus will receive students from Paris School of Business but also from other higher education institutions: Atelier de Sèvres, IESA Arts & Culture, le Cours Florent and Penninghen.

The state-of-the-art building will enhance the student experience and will allow us to build bridges and create more synergies with the other sister schools located on the campus that specialise in other disciplines. The campus will offer the following amenities:

A listed library

2 cafés

Coworking spaces

A fab lab

A MakerLab

A photo and video studio

A sound studio

A gym

Please find attached some 3D pictures of the future campus that will give you a preview of what the campus will look like.

We are really excited about this project and look forward to seeing you and your students from September 2025.

In order for them to secure an accommodation with ECLA, they need to fill in a form which we will communicate to them after receiving their application.  Commute time from both residences to our campus ranges from 30-40min. For reference, you will hereby find a brochure of the two residences we partnered with (ECLA Villejuif and ECLA Noisy-le-Grand).

content.about_text:
Useful pages for students:

+ syllabi link sent via email (Don’t forget to download them!)

“ESCE mainly uses English in all its offered exchange courses. It was nearly impossible to find any major class in French, as they only offered French language classes and some cultural classes such as real-life outings. However, these French courses weren't offered at Yonsei for credit transfer. When I registered for finance major classes, all were in English. They came as a package of 5 classes (18 ECTS) and not a single class to be added or removed. I contacted the ESCE office to ask if I could drop any classes, and they confirmed it was not possible.

In the course catalogue attached to this email, you can see that French classes are very limited. For major classes, the third year has a total of one French class, and the fourth and fifth years with all three majors are taught in English. Exchange courses have a total of two French classes, and all others are in English. Cultural classes have a total of two French classes. The rest are beginner French language classes. (Total of 5 French taught classes in the whole catalogue).”

link to courses syllabus:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1psd76y9LR_343scE4IFOx0D2WNM3s3hr

1, 수업 종류

크게 교환학생 전용 수업(EE)과 전공 수업으로 나누어 나누어져 있다. EE 수업에 해당되는 몇몇 프랑스어 및 프랑스 문화 수업을 제외하고는 모두 영어로 진행된다.

EE 수업 중 프랑스어로 진행되는 수업은 프랑스어 수업(A0, A1, A2, B1, B2 레벨 중 하나 선택), Zoom sur Paris (A2 레벨 요구), La France à 360° (B1-B2 레벨 요구)이다. 영어로 진행되는 프랑스 문화 수업인 French Civilization 수업, 스페인어 수업인 Spanish language 수업을 제외하고는 모두 경영 수업이며 전공 수업보다는 가벼운 수준이다.

전공 수업은 한 가지 전공을 선택하면 그 전공에 해당하는 약 6개의 과목들을 묶어서 전부 같이 듣게 된다. Export management, International Digital Marketing, Finance, Corporate Finance, Marketing, International Business and Development 등의 전공이 있다. 한 전공에 해당하는 과목을 전부 듣게 되면 18 ETC를 채울 수 있다. 주로 팀플이나 발표 위주의 수업이다.

수강 신청

출국 전 수강 신청 관련 메일이 온다. 실라버스를 읽고 원하는 수업을 선택해 온라인 사이트를 통해 수강신청을 한다. 연세대학교처럼 무슨 요일 몇 교시의 일정이 나와 있고 학생이 이에 맞춰서 시간표를 짜는게 아니라 수업만 선택하면 학교 쪽에서 분반을 알아서 조정해 준다. 개강 후 수강 변경 기간이 있기 때문에 관심있는 수업을 여러 개 신청해 두고 수업을 들어 본 후 드랍하거나 추가하는 것을 추천한다. 프랑스어 수업 같은 경우 첫 수업 때 교수님이 학생들의 실력을 확인하고 높은 레벨 혹은 낮은 레벨으로 변경할 것을 권유해주시기 때문에 자신이 어떤 레벨을 선택해야 할지 헷갈린다면 수업을 들어보고 변경하면 된다.

수업 평가 방식

20점 만점에 10점을 넘겨야 패스를 받을 수 있다. 평가 방식은 주로 시험이나 조별 과제이며 수업마다 상이하다. 한 학기에 과목 상관없이 15시간 이상 수업을 빠지게 되면 전체 점수에서 25%가 감점이 되는 규칙이 있다. 그렇기 때문에 결석이 15시간을 넘기지 않도록 출결 관리가 필요할 것이다. 주의할 점은 ESCE는 수업 시간표가 대체적으로 정해져 있기는 하지만 갑자기 시간이나 날짜가 바뀌는 경우가 많기 때문에 학기 중 여행 등의 계획을 짤 때 수업 일정을 잘 확인하여야 한다는 것이다.

Necessary steps 의 하위 항목 최하단에 표시된 Complete application form/ Email confirmation of complete application received은 지원자가 아닌 상대교 담당자가 서류 검토 후 체크하는 항목이므로, 그 위의 모든 서류들을 제출하셨다면 더 이상 추가로 진행할 사항은 없고 2차 서류 제출이 완료된 것이 맞습니다. 상대교의 서류 확인이 완료될 때까지 기다리시면 됩니다.

The "Complete application form/ Email confirmation of complete application received" item listed at the bottom of the "Necessary steps" section is meant to be checked by the staff at ESCE after reviewing the documents, not by the applicant. If you have submitted all the documents listed above, there are no further steps for you to take, and your application submission is complete. Please wait for ESCE to finish reviewing your documents.

🏡: Maison de la Coree is highly recommended for your accommodation if possible. We know that finding a good residence in Paris is quite difficult and expensive. Maison de la Coree is a safe, clean and reasonable-priced residence in Paris. You can also meet a lot of Koreans and other foreign friends in Cite Universitaire. There are various programs held in Cite such as a language exchange program and parties!

📱: Also, always watch out for your belongings, especially your phones and purses.

🛍️: There is the ‘Westfield’ near ESCE, so you can go and grab something in lunch time or you can do shopping.

🎨: Museums are FREE with your student VISA. Use it well.

🐔: Profitez des week-ends et de vos temps libres pour voyager beaucoup ! Paris est tellement une ville merveilleuse et n’est pas loin des autres pays europeens. Vous ne parlez pas francais ? Ne vous inquietez pas !!

content.about_text:
Get to know us

The University of Jyvaskyla is an internationally renowned research university. We are one of the largest and most popular multidisciplinary universities in Finland!

Welcome to study at the University of Jyväskylä! We have been providing education since 1863, starting with teacher education and expanding into a wide range of disciplines. Jyväskylä is the right size for a city that is a pleasure to visit, but also a pleasure to stay in. Build your future in Finland, with the cutting-edge research and education our university offers. Our international university community is easy to join, both as a student and as an employee.

Rector Keijo Hämäläinen

University of Jyväskylä (JYU) is an internationally ranked research university. We are one of the largest and most popular multidisciplinary universities in Finland. JYU provides a genuinely supportive and enjoyable environment for studying. Our university has 15 000 students from about 100 countries. We believe that interaction with people from different cultures and backgrounds is an essential part of academic studies and the university experience. We are therefore committed to promoting international cooperation.

Study experience at JYU is shown as excellent results in the International Student Barometer (ISB) in autumn 2017: Over 90% of our international respondents are satisfied with their study experience. In the survey, JYU's ratings for satisfaction with living were the best worldwide and satisfaction with
teaching was the highest in Finland. ISB also shows our success in welcoming our international students, as satisfaction with arrival experiences and conditions topped other European universities.

The University of Jyväskylä is pleased to welcome you to study with us for a semester or two as an exchange student! The information on these pages is designed to walk you through our online application process and offer you information on the services available to exchange students.

Fact Sheet

content.accommodation:
The annual application periods for incoming exchange students are:

For the academic year or autumn semester

April 1 - April 15: citizens of non-EU countries/EEA-member states (by midnight Helsinki local time).

April 1 - May 15: citizens of EU/EEA-member states and Switzerland (by midnight Helsinki local time).

For the spring semester

September 1 - September 30: citizens of non-EU countries/EEA-member states (by midnight Helsinki local time).

September 1 - October 15: citizens of EU/EEA-member states and Switzerland (by midnight Helsinki local time).

If you are a citizen of a non-EU country, please refer to our section "Residence Permit for Exchange and Visiting Students" on our website for more information regarding the procedures to acquire a residence permit for your studies in Finland. Please remember to apply according to the non-EU application deadline if you are a non-EU citizen enrolled at an EU institution.

The application period for visiting students coming to do supervised research or a traineeship is ongoing. Students should apply two months prior to their stay the latest. Please note that visiting students wishing to attend courses have the same application period and deadline as exchange students (see above). For studies beginning in the autumn semester, please do not apply before April 1st.

Please note that late applications will not be considered!

Exchange duration can be found on page The Academic Year and Teaching Periods.

The exchange period includes 2-3 day long, mandatory orientation week.

content.about_text: 📁 Factsheet and documents
content.campus_location:
Finland scores high in international comparisons in education, safety and quality of life. As a place to study and live, it's hard to beat! The beautiful Aalto campus locates next to the sea and nature reserves, yet within excellent connections in the capital area. Learn more about our campus and country!

Aalto University’s campus is located in the Helsinki metropolitan area, close to both nature as well as the city buzz. The campus area offers an ideal environment for living, studying and leisure. Get to know the campus!

The Aalto campus is located in Otaniemi, in the city of Espoo. Otaniemi is home to a lively community, and it could be even described as a small town in itself with excellent services from study hubs to restaurants and cafes, stores and sports facilities. The location is a perfect balance of vivid city life and nature: hop on a metro and travel to the heart of Helsinki in less than 15 minutes or head to the neighbouring nature reserve to catch some fresh air.

Google Maps

Campus Map

Virtual Campus Tour

📔 Module(Course) Catalogue

At the Mikkeli Campus the academic year is divided into 15 threeweek modules and courses are offered throughout the year, also during the summer. The students study one course at the time in each 3-week module. For a typical module, classes for a 6 ECTS course are scheduled for three hours a day, five days a week, for a total of 45 contact hours, which includes examinations. Due to the intensive modular structure, attendance is mandatory and only few absences are allowed per course.

In Aalto University the academic year has been divided into 5 study periods. Periods I and II take place in the autumn term and periods III, IV and V in the spring term.

Note that if you have been nominated to study here for the autumn term you can only choose courses organised in the periods I and II (you cannot continue for the period III). Spring term applicants can choose courses held in the periods III, IV and V.

The general information of every major/programme is available at Academic catalogues 2024-2026

You can see further information on each course in Sisu

The exchange agreements between Aalto University and partner universities are made for a specific school or field of study at Aalto. This means that you should apply to and choose courses mainly from that specific school or field of study.

For example, if your home university has an exchange agreement with the School of Chemical Engineering, you cannot apply to study in the School of Arts, Design and Architecture. If you are unsure how to select the courses, please contact the exchange coordinator in the Aalto school you have been nominated to.

In addition, you are welcome to take some interdisciplinary studies such as University Wide Studies! Aalto University offers a unique possibility to try something different from your own field of studies. Please read more below.

Exchange students follow the same courses as degree students. Most of the courses lectured in English are available for you in the school you have been nominated to. However, there are courses that have some restrictions or quotas. The requirements are usually mentioned in the course description. Certain courses can sometimes be available for certain majors only or master's level students only etc. Large number of master level courses are taught in English, however, bachelor level students are allowed to take master level courses provided that prerequisites are met.

All the students are required to have a feasible Learning Agreement/Study Plan. It is important to remember that the Learning Agreement/Study Plan is preliminary and some changes after arrival are possible. Please note that you might not be accepted to all courses you have chosen. It is good to have some alternative courses in mind and you have to be prepared to make some changes to your original Learning Agreement/Study Plan.

When you choose courses for your Learning Agreement/Study Plan, the updated course information might not be available yet. In this case, the study plan is made according to the course information of the previous academic year. You can find the course list for each Aalto school below.

When planning your exchange period please remember that incoming exchange students are expected to study full time while at Aalto and to pass the courses with good grades. Full workload at Aalto is 30 ECTS credits per term.

Aalto University School of Arts, Design and Architecture offers courses in English for exchange students both on Bachelor and Master level.
Exchange students at Aalto University are expected to study full-time while at Aalto. A full workload is equivalent to 30 ETCS credits per semester (autumn or spring semester) or 60 ETCS credits per academic year.

When planning your studies, note that the teaching periods I and II are during the autumn term, and the teaching periods III, IV and V during the spring term.

You should mainly choose courses in the major you have been nominated for as an exchange student.

General Art Studies

Exchange students who have been accepted to the School of Arts, Design and Architecture may choose general art and theory courses offered by the Transdisclipinary Arts Studies (TAITE). Please note that they are very popular and have limited intake, and the places for these courses are drawn. You can plan to take any of them, but you may not be able to get a place in the course you would like to take.

Courses from other majors

Exchange students should select courses of the major they have been accepted to. Taking courses from other majors or departments is sometimes possible. However, this always needs to be discussed with the study coordinators of both programmes first and needs to be confirmed by the teacher.

University Wide Studies

Exchange students may choose courses in the selection of University Wide Studies that provide a multidisciplinary perspective on a form of studying where engineering and art can meet business and management.

Language courses

Finnish courses for exchange students and other language courses are organized by the Language Centre.

Department specific information

Department of Architecture

Exchange students at the Department of Architecture will be informed in detail about all study options available during the Studio Info Sessions, where all available studio courses will be introduced, taking place at the beginning of each term. Exchange students can participate in 1 studio course / term.

Application for the studio course is possible after the studio presentations. In addition to this, students may select other courses in the field of Architecture.

The exchange students from Yonsei University can take businessclasses in Mikkeli only (International Business).

At Aalto Mikkeli, the courses are organized in intensive three weeks modules and in each module, you are studying one course five days a week, three hours a day. All the course related work will be done during the module and there are no separate exam periods. Below you can find the course schedule for exchange students, where you can find the course description and learning outcomes for the courses we offer for exchange students.

All the courses in our programme are 6 ECTS credits (exception to this: Data Visualization and Storytelling for Business is 4 ECTS). There are 45 contact hours (including exams) and the calculated student working hours per course are 160 h.

In the School of Chemical Engineering master programmes offered are Master’s programme in Chemistry and Materials Science, Master’s programme in Bioproducts Engineering, Master’s programme in Chemical and Metallurgical Engineering and Master’s programme in Biotechnology. Exchange students do not need to follow the curriculum of one programme only but are allowed to choose courses from the whole variety of the course offering of the school (provided that prerequisites are met).

;ost courses taught in English are on the Master's level, but available for both Master and Bachelor level students provided that the prerequisites are met.  Exchange students can choose courses offered in English at the School of Chemical Engineering freely as long as they have the required prerequisites, knowledge and skills to attend the course. However, when choosing courses from many different majors it may cause some overlaps in course timetables.

In some cases, there may be restrictions in participation for certain courses (e.g. course availability restricted only for students of certain major or priority given to the degree students of the school), but applicant will be informed of these once their learning agreement/study plan has been checked.

Below you can find a list of courses offered in English at Aalto CHEM. Use the list as a starting point for making your Learning Agreement/study plan, but please note that it is subject to change. Thus, you need to be prepared to make changes upon arrival due to overlaps in schedule or course cancellations.

At least 2/3 of the courses selected must be from the School of Chemical Engineering in order to be admitted to this school. 1/3 of the courses can be selected from the other three Aalto University engineering schools (School of Science, School of Engineering and the School of Electrical Engineering) or from the Language Centre or from the interdisciplinary course offering (e.g. UWS-courses).

Courses from the Aalto University School of Business and the School of Arts, Design and Architecture can not be selected, apart from the Aalto cross-school courses, UWS and interdisciplinary courses.

A full study load is approximately 1600 hours per academic year which equals to 60 ECTS (= 60 cr). We expect incoming exchange students to study full time while at Aalto and to pass the courses with good grades. Full workload at Aalto is 30 ECTS credits for one semester.

Exchange students can choose courses from the School of Electrical Engineering (ELEC) quite freely as long as they have the required knowledge and skills to attend the course. In some cases, there may be restrictions, but applicants will be informed of these once their Learning Agreement/Study Plan has been checked.

The majority of courses offered in English at ELEC are master-level courses. However, we also offer bachelor-level courses in English and if you are a bachelor-level student and in the final stages of your degree (esp. fourth-year students), you are free to take courses at master-level if you fulfill their pre-knowledge requirements. Because the choice of bachelor-level courses in English is limited, we recommend that bachelor students apply to the Aalto University School of Electrical Engineering only in the final year(s) of their degree. Bachelor's students in the beginning of their studies may find it problematic to find suitable courses in English.

Instructions for preparing your learning agreement/study plan

In order to be admitted to the School of Electrical Engineering, 2/3 of the engineering courses in your Learning Agreement/Study Plan must be selected from the School of Electrical Engineering. 1/3 of the courses can be selected from the other three Aalto University engineering schools (School of Science, School of Engineering and School of Chemical Engineering). In addition to engineering courses, you can select interdisciplinary courses and courses offered by the Language Centre.

Courses from the Aalto University School of Business and the School of Arts, Design and Architecture cannot be selected, apart from the University Wide Studies and interdisciplinary courses.

Check the language of instruction and the term when the course is offered (especially if you are applying for one term only). You can find this information by clicking on the course code on the course list above. This will take you to Sisu portal where you can read more about the course.

Make sure that you have the prerequisite skills and knowledge required for the course. We will check your course choices preliminarily, but in the end, the student is responsible for checking the course prerequisites

Instructions for selecting courses - Making your Learning Agreement / Study plan

You should choose at least 2/3 of the courses from one programme at the School of Engineering, to be admitted to this school for exchange. This is approximately 20 credits.

The remaining 1/3 of the courses (10 credits) can be selected from another programme at the School of Engineering, from the other three Aalto University engineering schools (School of Chemical Engineering, School of Electrical Engineering and School of Science), from the Language Centre or from the interdisciplinary course offering.

Courses in Real Estate Economics (course code REC-Exxxx) and Water and Environmental Engineering (course code WAT-Exxxx) are only available to exchange students in those programmes.

Courses from School of Arts, Design and Architecture and School of Business cannot be selected, apart from the Aalto cross-school courses, University Wide Studies (UWS) and interdisciplinary courses.

It’s important to plan for 30 credits when applying so that once you’re registering for the courses and make changes you will have enough credits (usually 25-30 credits). There is no upper limit to credits you can take, but 30 credits is considered full-time studying at Aalto and we do not recommend exceeding this due to course overlaps, workload and availability of spaces on courses.

Please note that we cannot guarantee places on individual courses as they are subject to fulfilment of certain preconditions, availability, limitation to intake and timetabling factors. Therefore, you need to be prepared to adjust your Learning Agreement / Study plan when registering for courses.

Checking course details

Make sure that you have the prerequisite skills and knowledge required for the course. We will check your course choices preliminarily, but in the end you are responsible for checking the course prerequisites.

How do I know if I have the background knowledge required if I haven't completed the prerequisite course(s) at Aalto?> Compare the prerequisite course details to the courses you have completed in your home university.

Periods I and II refer to teaching periods of the autumn term (September - December), and periods III and IV and V refer to the teaching periods of the spring term (January - June). Teaching periods include examinations during the last week of the period.

Check carefully the language of instruction and the term when the course is offered (especially if you are applying for one term only).

The extent of the courses is given in ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) credits. One credit corresponds to 27 hours of work, including lectures and other forms of instruction, exercises, seminars and independent work. The scope of a course varies between 1-15 credits depending on the content of the course.

Full workload is approximately 1600 hours per academic year which equals to 60 credits, which makes it 30 credits for one term. We expect incoming exchange students to study full time while at Aalto and to pass the courses with good grades.

The School of Science offers courses for exchange students in the following fields of study:

Computer, Communication and Information Sciences

Industrial Engineering and Management

Engineering Physics

Mathematics and Operations Research

Life Science Technologies, Biomedical Engineering, Human Neuroscience and Technology

Exchange students to Aalto are expected to study full time while at Aalto and pass the courses with good grades. Full workload is equivalent to 30 ECTS credits per term or 60 ECTS per academic year.

Please select at least 2/3 of the courses (amount of credits) from one field of study at the School of Science:

Computer Science (course code starts with CS-)

Industrial Engineering and Management (course code starts with TU-)

Engineering Physics (course code starts with PHYS-)

Mathematics and Operations Research (course code starts with MS-)

Biomedical Engineering and Neuroscience (course code starts with NBE-)

The rest 1/3 of courses may include:

Courses from other fields of study offered by the School of Science

Courses from other technical schools (the School of Chemical Engineering, School of Electrical Engineering and the School of Engineering)

Language and communication courses, including Finnish courses for international students

Interdisciplinary courses, such as:

Aalto Ventures Program (AVP) courses - AVP courses cover important aspects of entrepreneurship; leadership, design thinking, finance, innovation, start-ups, prototyping, storytelling and much more.

Aaltonaut courses - Aaltonaut is Aalto University’s Bachelor’s Minor Programme on Interdisciplinary Product Development. Aaltonaut courses rely on problem-based learning as well as interdisciplinary team-work in hands-on projects. The general goals of the Aaltonaut programme are creating collaboration between different disciplines, refining teamwork and communication skills and reinforcing the entrepreneurial attitude.

Creative Sustainability courses - Creative Sustainability programme offers courses which approach sustainability from different perspectives and disciplinary angles. Most courses are open to Aalto University exchange students. Important: Courses are popular and of limited size. Please make sure to enroll as early as possible and make alternative study plans.

University Wide Studies -courses

School of Business -courses, if the course is available for all Aalto students according to the course description available in sisu.aalto.fi

Please note that courses from the Aalto University the School of Arts, Design and Architecture cannot be selected due to very limited intake. School of Business -courses can be selected only if it is mentioned in the course description that the course is open for all Aalto students.

When choosing courses, please check carefully the prerequisite skills, language of instruction and the period when the course is offered.

When you choose courses for your learning agreement / study plan, the detailed course information might not be available yet. In this case, the study plan is made according to the course information of the previous academic year.

Please be prepared to modify your study plan upon arrival. You might have to remove some courses due to conflict in schedule, difficulty level of the course, limited availability of places in the course or other reasons, and respectively select new courses. Study plan / learning agreement is not a course registration! You will register for courses in Sisu before or upon arrival. For course registration you need Aalto username and password, which you will receive upon enrolling.

In some cases, there may be restrictions in participation for certain courses (e.g. the amount of students can be limited or priority given to degree students).

In the online application form, under “Studies”, you are asked to select the “Stay opportunity”. Sometimes there are several options for your home institution and the following options may be included in the list of possible study programmes, so select which one is the programme at Aalto University School of Science you are applying for:

SCI (CS): School of Science, Computer Science

SCI (IEM): School of Science, Industrial Engineering and Management

SCI (PHYS): School of Science, Physics

SCI (MATH): School of Science, Mathematics

SCI (LIFE): School of Science, Life Science Technologies / Bioengineering
content.accommodation:
Accommodation is arranged by the local student housing company, MOAS Ltd. Students apply for the accommodation through MOAS(www.moas.fi). Normally students stay in shared apartments near the campus.
In shared apartments, students will have their own lockable bedroom and share the kitchen and bathroom with 1–2 students. There are also single apartments available. The rent varies from 280–335 euros for a shared apartment and approximately 430 euros for a single apartment (the rent includes furniture, electricity, water, Internet, use of laundry and sauna). A monthly budget for exchange students would be approximately 600–800 euros per month (including accommodation, living expenses, course materials and personal expenses).

content.about_text:
Thus, at Aalborg University we would like to attract more exchange students, so if you have students wondering about:

Are there any exchange courses that match my field of study

How is a semester structed (exams/group work etc.)

What about the student life/Accommodation

Information about Denmark

Other

We are not more than a TEAMS meeting away - your students are more than welcome to send an email or book a meeting:

incoming-student@aau.dk or book an online session for guidance

📁 Factsheet and documents

What sets Aalborg University apart from the other great universities?

At Aalborg University, every semester, students will conduct a problem-based project report in groups of 4-7 students. This report will be based on AAU’s model of learning – The Aalborg Model (PBL model). The model focuses on allowing students to gain knowledge and skills independently, by working towards a result that can be used in the ‘real world’. Aalborg University’s unique study method, The Aalborg Model for Problem-Based Learning (PBL) is highly respected both nationally and internationally.

Aalborg University is very famous for its take on the ‘Problem Based Learning’ method or PBL method for short. The competencies acquired through PBL are highly recognized in the business community

Informal student-teacher relationship, students and professors are on a first-name basis and you will always be encouraged to speak your mind during lectures

Friendships through group work and the Buddy Programme

You can find all courses and other relevant information here: www.exchange.aau.dk

Aalborg University differs from other universities especially due to the PBL model.

AAU partners with companies and public institutions so AAU students work closely together with collaborators in companies and public institutions to solve real-life issues.

Therefore, students at AAU acquire important qualifications and strengthen their existing qualifications for future jobs. And they are never alone, as they will be part of a group with other AAU students throughout the period.

The PBL in short is described by students in the videos below:

PBL – working with theory in practice together with peers - YouTube

Problem Based Project Work (PBL) at Aalborg University (AAU) - YouTube

Aalborg University ranks as best University in Europe, within the field of Engineering and as no. 8 in the world according to US News World Ranking. In THE Impact Ranking, Aalborg University is ranked as the worlds best university at the SDG 4: Quality Education.

Top-ranked university - AAU is ranked as the best university within the field of Engineering in Europe and in the top 2% of all universities in the world (https://www.usnews.com/education/best-global-universities/aalborg-university-501276)

PBL-university, UNESCO has placed its only Danish Chair in PBL at AAU thus we guarantee your students will benefit from this unique learning method at AAU Problem Based Learning (PBL) at Aalborg University (AAU) - Aalborg University

Affordable housing - Our International Accommodation Office helps exchange students get a room, apartment, or dormitory at one of Aalborg University’s many apartments in and around the city of Aalborg.

Aalborg University is very famous for its take on the ‘Problem Based Learning’ method or PBL method for short. The competencies acquired through PBL are highly recognized in the business community

Informal student-teacher relationship, students and professors are on a first-name basis and you will always be encouraged to speak your mind during lectures

Friendships through group work and the Buddy Programme

Intensive Introduction Programme aimed specifically at helping international students to get a good and safe start at AAU. Find the programme: Introduction Programme - Aalborg University (aau.dk)

The city of Aalborg has a lot to offer any exchange student. A stable of Danish culture is our association’s life, whether athletic or social associations. You can find many athletic- and social associations with a wide range of interests, that also welcome internationals.

Many surveys show that Scandinavia countries, Denmark included, are regarded among the safest countries in the world, with low crime rates, high mutual trust, affordable housing, and free health care. All benefits your students will enjoy as exchange students at AAU.

Students will be entitled to our great healthcare system for the duration of their stay. Similarly, the students will experience the Danish values; individual freedom, freedom of speech, equal rights and possibilities for everyone, solidarity, respect, and tolerance which are all central values in Denmark.

Aalborg is the fourth largest city in Denmark with just over 200.000 inhabitants and with a great focus on attracting internationals and hosting one of the biggest carnivals in Europe.

Our exchange courses are part of existing study programmes. The exchange courses consist of:

3-5 predetermined modules, including a project module (free-topic)

For some of the exchange courses there is also a small selection of elective modules

Semesters are pre-planned and as stated in the curriculum. Thus, mixing and matching different courses and programmes is impossible.

You can find detailed information on a section of guest and exchange courses with only specific semesters taught in English here:

Find your exchange courses at AAU

We’ll like to highlight that Aalborg University (AAU) from this Fall semester 2025 offers exchange courses within the Economics program.

AAU are famous for our Problem Based Learning (PBL) Model & groupwork, which is a big advantage when studying within Economics Programs. The students work together in groups with the aim of solving real-life problems and invent new solutions.

Your students can choose exchange course at either the bachelor’s or master’s level in Economics.

content.accommodation:
UCLouvain Accommodation :

Getting a Private accommodation :

Two types of UCLouvain accommodations : Kot & KAP(Kot à projet)

“It consists of single bedrooms with common areas i.e. kitchen, living room, bathroom(s) /shower(s), and toilet(s). A “kot” can be pre-booked in advance through the UCLouvain “Service des Logements” (UCLouvain Housing Service). It gives you the possibility to live with Belgian students in a shared apartment. A UCLouvain kot is usually less expensive than other housing solutions and most of the rooms are on campus or in the neighborhood.”

“In a KAP, you will share a common interest with your housemates and work on a project during your exchange period. All KAPs are supervised by the university. Those rooms must also be booked through the Service des Logements (UCLouvain housing service).”

Warning :

Finding a place to stay in Louvain-la-Neuve while still in your home country can be quite difficult, so LSM advises you to start your search for accommodation as soon as possible

universities.available_areas:
* Faculty of Economics and Business Administration

* Students must choose 1 campus among Campus Brussels/Leuven/Antwerp. 
- Antwerp campus has management and business courses on bachelor & Master level. Courses on the bachelor level only available in the first semester (for example: finance, accounting and harbour logistics). Groups tend to be small and teach hands-on.
- Brussels campus has a strong focus on business administration and management (both bachelor and master entirely in English with additional minors).
- Leuven campus has a smaller offer for bachelor students but has a large offer for master students with many interesting courses in economics, business economics and business engineering. Students who want to study on the Leuven campus need to be strong in Mathematics & statistics as courses tend to be very analytical. 

Students are allowed to take maximum 6 ECTS at another campus then their main campus
universities.restricted_areas: Students selected at the faculty of Economics & Business, need to take the majority of the courses at this faculty, however they are allowed to take maximum 2 courses from another faculty, but only after approval from the student advisor at the faculty of Economics & Business who'll contact the coordinator at the other faculty.

content.about_text:
Belgium offers the perfect base to explore and experience Europe in all its variety. Make the most of Belgium’s central location and excellent transportation links to travel round.

FEB offers a unique behind the scenes look at the workings of the EU through its Business and Economics in Europe programme, especially designed for overseas exchange students.

Courses in European politics, policies, law, decision making & business

Pick and choose courses across campuses (Brussels, Antwerp, Leuven)

Study visits to EU institutions

Pick up at airport

A selection of social/cultural activities

More information can be found on our webpage “Business and Economics in Europe”.

Students who enroll for this programme can only take the courses which are mentioned in our flyer.

Have you read the testimonials and want to find out more about coming on exchange at FEB KU Leuven? Read all about it in our info brochure for incoming exchange students.

Required documents for the application:

• Photo
• Copy identity card/passport
• A Transcript of Records (ToR) in English of all the previous courses/years showing the number of credits you obtained so far. A min. of 60 ECTS credits need to be obtained before July.
• Learning agreement
• non-native English speakers: one of the documents listed below as proof of B2 (bachelor) or C1 (master) proficiency in English:
• result of official test (TOEFL, IELTS, etc.)
• proof by your university that you are registered for an English-taught programme
• certificate from your university stating your level in English

Please read our factsheet 2024-2025

More details on the application procedure can be found here.

Courses Offered to Exchange Students

Course description, course list and guidelinesFull list of courses open to bachelor/undergraduate exchange students and master/graduateexchange students on all FEB campuses:

Ready to fall in love?

If an exchange experience is on your bucket list, but you’re still narrowing down your
destinations, we can tell you here and now that you’ll feel right at home in Ghent and at

The city is best explored by walking the streets, exploring the Museum of Fine Arts or grabbing a beer in a cosy pub, … But we’ve come up with an alternative that is as close to the real thing as it gets! Happy exploring!

content.accommodation:
The university does not own any halls of residence. Students are therefore responsible for organising accommodation for their stay in Vienna themselves. If you want to book a room in a student dorm, please check the following websites:

WIHAST
Web: http://www.wihast.at
E-mail: info@wihast.at

OeAD Housing Office
Web: https://housing.oead.at/en/
E-mail: housing@oead.ac.at

For more details and alternatives please refer to our website(above).

universities.available_areas:
All ANU Academic Colleges are avialable for exchange students to take courses under. Concurrent multifaculty study is available. Study outside of a student's home university major is allowed as long as students meet any prerequisities required to undertake the desired courses. 

Exchange studetns are also eligible to apply to undertake the Australian National Interships Program (ANIP) and the College fo Bsuiness and Economics (CBE) Special Industry Project (SIP). Separate application deadlines apply. Contact global.programs@anu.edu.au for more infromation. 

ANIP - https://anip.anu.edu.au/
SIP - https://cbe.anu.edu.au/current-students/opportunities-and-careers/special-industry-project-course
universities.restricted_areas:
• Courses offered by the ANU Medical School are not available.
• Art studio, music, and design courses are limited.
• Law courses are only available to students who are enrolled in a law degree at their home institution.
• All students must meet the prerequisite requirements for the courses they wish to take.
universities.admission_notes: 1 year of study (48 ANU units equivalent) at home university completed upon commencemnt at ANU. Students must meet the English language requirements set out in the policy above.

universities.english_notes: Min of 22 in reading and writing
universities.available_areas: Undergraduate courses from all faculties
universities.admission_notes:
1) Applicants must have completed at least one academic year of undergraduate studies and must be a registered undergraduate in their home university throughout the study term
2) Students from non-English-speaking countries or from institutions whereby the language of instruction is not English, must fulfil at least one of the listed English language proficiency certifications.:

1)	TOEFL PBT: Min. of 583 (with min. 5.5 for writing & 50 for reading) OR
2)	TOEFL iBT: Min of 93 (with min. 22 for reading & 22 for writing) OR
3)	IELTS: Min. of 7.0 (with min. 7.0 for reading & 6.5 for writing) OR
4)	C1 Advanced: Min. of 185 (with min. of 185 for reading and 176 for writing)
