universities.admission_notes: First- and second-year undergraduate students in four-year degree programs are not eligible for USTEP. Students enrolled in three-year undergraduate programs must have completed at least 1.5 years of their program before arriving at UTokyo.

universities.language_other_notes:
1. Japan-Okinawa Studies Course: Daily conversation level of Japanese or English
2. Glocal Leadership Course: Daily conversation level of Japanese or English (CEFR A2)
3. Japanese Language Teacher Training Course: JLPT N2 equivalent or above
4. Major Studies Course: JLPT N2 equivalent or above is required basically. However, if the applicant can find an academic adviser who can give the guidance in a foreign language they wish to use, they can conduct their research in the foreign language (English, Thai, etc.). Please contact the Global Education Section in advance.
universities.available_areas: Students can apply for any faculties. However, the Faculty of Medicine seldom accepts international students because their field of study requires highly specialized knowledge, and most of the courses are already full of Japanese students.

universities.admission_notes:
1. The student's institution must be affiliated with the University of the Ryukyus or be a partner of the UMAP or ISEP.
2. The student must either be enrolled as a fulltime undergraduate student who has completed his/her first year of study at his/her home institution or be matriculated as a graduate student. Junior college students who apply must be in their final year of study.
3. The students must be proficient in Japanese at the level required by each given course.
4. The students must have a non-Japanese passport.

Fudan provides three different exchange programs. Students must choose ONLY ONE of them:
1. Chinese Language Program (taking Chinese Language courses at
ICES ONLY)
2. General Advanced Program (taking undergraduate-level courses)
In general, General Advanced Program students CAN NOT take Chinese Language courses at ICES. Limited Chinese Language courses at ICES and non-credit Chinese language training classes might be available in this program, but there is NO guarantee that they will be available every semester. Registered Chinese Language courses at ICES
will appear on your official transcript, but non-credit Chinese language training classes will NOT.
Undergraduate-level courses in basic medical sciences, clinical medicine, and nursing are not available.
English-taught Course lists: https://ogp.fudan.edu.cn/16092/list.htm
3. Senior Advanced Program (taking EMA courses ONLY: English-taught graduate-level courses)
EMA course lists: https://ogp.fudan.edu.cn/16092/list.htm
Here are the schools/departments that offer EMA courses: Department of Chinese Language and Literature, School of Economics, School of International Relations and Public Affairs, School of Philosophy. Other schools/departments DO NOT offer EMA courses.

shared kitchen and living room). Dormitory rooms include a mandatory meal plan.

Important application dates and deadlines are available on the Residence Services website. UVic on campus
residence spaces are limited and competitive. Placement is not guaranteed for all applicants:
https://www.uvic.ca/residence/future-residents/apply/undergraduate/index.php 

Students with specialized housing requirements or who require priority admittance to housing outside
of current admissions practices or eligibility criteria, based on restrictions or limitations resulting from
disability, chronic health conditions, dietary restrictions, or other considerations, are asked to submit a
Priority Consideration Request (PCR) as part of their housing application.

universities.admission_notes:
Students are highly encouraged to list 3-5 placement preferences in their application. We try to honor every student’s first preference but due to the number of applications received and the availability at each institution, we are not always able to honor the first preference.
If the student is not placed at their first-choice school, this does not mean they were “rejected” from that school. It simply means that, due to a variety of factors, we were unable to accommodate their first-choice request and their application was forwarded to the next available placement preference.

universities.available_areas:
Exchange students are allowed to take any courses at WWU. However, there are some limitations to enrollment in the following areas: College of Business courses, Environmental Studies, Chemistry, Computer Science, Political Science, Biology, Geology, Physical Education, Kinesiology, Health and Recreation, Communications, English, Psychology, Education and Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages (TESOL) courses. Audition or portfolio is required for admission to some Music and Art classes.

Regarding Physical Education classes: All Physical Education classes have additional charges. This means that the normal exchange tuition waivers provided by WWU will not cover PE classes. So, PE classes will not be an option unless students choose to pay the additional charge per credit fee themselves.
Regarding online classes: Online courses are not available for exchange students. Please do not register for online courses.
There is no guarantee of being able to enroll in all desired courses, so flexibility is required and the courses requested may not be the actual courses taken while at WWU. Course enrollment is often at the discretion of the course instructor and/or academic department, or depends on the space available in any given course. Information about academic departments https://www.wwu.edu/department-directory and courses offered, https://admin.wwu.edu/pls/wwis/wwsktime.SelClass .

universities.available_areas:
Incoming exchange students are only permitted to take courses through the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts(LSA); https://lsa.umich.edu/lsa/academics/courses.html
Incoming exchange students are not permitted to take courses in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS). 

Incoming exchange students are encouraged to stay in UM on-campus housing. Unfortunately, UM on-campus housing is not guaranteed and it is extremely difficult for exchange students to secure a spot in on-campus housing. Interested students should apply for UM on-campus housing, but should also look for off-campus housing options in the event that they cannot secure on-campus housing. 

Only undergraduate students may apply for the exchange program.
universities.restricted_areas:
In general, exchange students should plan on selecting undergraduate coursework from the College of Literature, Science, and Arts (LSA). Access to courses in other units is severely limited and requires special permission and overrides, which may not be approved.
 Qualified students apply to the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts and may enroll in courses from any department in the college. Courses in Economics, Computer Science, Communication Studies, Psychology, and Political Science are particularly difficult to enroll in. CGIS cannot guarantee enrollment in any specific courses, especially from these four departments. Therefore, students should not depend on enrollment in courses primarily from these departments.

content.about_text:
Information Session

Promotional Videos

Fun Facts

The University began as an agricultural college and there is a working farm on campus today, along with the UM Dairy, which churns out fresh ice cream all year long!

The Point of Failure on South Campus has lines that point to where the old buildings of the university once stood before the fire in 1912. It is said that if you step on the Point of Failure, you won’t graduate in four years.

One of UMD's famous graduates is Jim Henson. He is the creator of The Muppets and there is a statue of him and Kermit the Frog right outside of The Stamp building on campus.

Finding UC Courses

In addition to using the search tool, see Majors Closed to Reciprocity Students.

Reciprocity students may not be able to enroll in impacted (very popular) courses, as UC departments prioritize UC degree-seeking students. Students from partner institutions who do not meet the minimum language requirements to study at Towson on exchange may take courses at our English Language Center (ELC) prior to their semester or full year on exchange. This is called the ELC + International Exchange program. Attending the ELC for one or more semesters allows them to improve proficiency in reading, writing and speaking in preparation for regular university courses on exchange.  To apply simply select the ELC + International Exchange option on the International Exchange Student Application.

content.about_text: Fact Sheet

universities.university_name: New York University - Stern School of Business
universities.available_areas:
As an exchange student, you can take a wide array of courses. However, you cannot register for more than two finance classes or two economics courses (see note below) or any graduate level (MBA) courses, and may only take one non-Stern (i.e. non-business) class. This non-business class can be taken from the College of Arts and Science (CAS). Exceptions to the one non-Stern class rule will only be made for Economics classes. Our Economics courses are taught between Stern and the College of Arts and Science (CAS), and you may take a maximum of two Economics classes while on exchange. Your Economics classes can either be both at Stern, both at CAS or one from Stern and one from CAS. If you select two Economics classes from CAS, the remainder of your courses must be taken at Stern.

You can use the Albert Course Search to view course offerings by semester. Course descriptions are available in the Stern Undergraduate Bulletin, the Stern Course Index, and the College of Arts and Science (CAS) Bulletin.

universities.restricted_areas:
Academic Restrictions
Students should have significant academic preparation in the discipline of their chosen major/program at Cornell. Students will take at least 50% of their courses in the department or college that is hosting them. Other courses may come from across the college or university.
Undergraduate students may apply to any undergraduate college covered by the exchange agreement. Generally, undergraduate exchange students are not approved to take graduate-level courses.
 
 Exchange students will be permitted to enroll in any course for which they are qualified, subject to meeting admission requirements, prerequisites, and timetable and capacity constraints at Cornell. However, there are specific academic restrictions to bear in mind: 
 
 Exchange students are unable to take courses in Computer Science (CS). Related subjects may be found in other departments (Electrical and Computer Engineering; Information Science, Systems, and Technology; or Operations Research and Information Engineering).
 Only students in business degree programs can apply to the Dyson School of Applied Economics and Management or the Nolan School of Hotel Administration which are both located in our College of Business.
 Studio classes (architecture, art, design, and fashion) are typically not available to students through general exchange agreements.
 Courses associated with professional graduate degree programs (MBA, MEng, MPS, MPH, etc.) are not available to non-degree students; in general, students should not expect to take graduate (MA, MS, PhD) courses. You will find this information and the additional information in our Fact Sheet.
universities.admission_notes:
Students entering Cornell for a full academic or calendar year are expected to maintain good academic standing. Good academic standing for incoming exchange and visiting students is defined as:

·         Earn a term GPA of 2.0 or higher in their first term at Cornell.

·         Successfully completing a minimum of 12 academic credits. To complete course credits, students must receive a letter grade of “D-” or higher, OR an “S” in a class taken S/U.

Failure to maintain good academic standing for your first term at Cornell will result in dismissal from the program.

universities.english_notes:
(minumum score of 21 points in each section)
 *Some departments and faculties have higher English language proficiency requirments for graduate/postgraduate students than those shown through this sheet. It is your responsibility to determind, before applying, the minimum English language profiency requirements that you will need to demonstrate.
universities.available_areas:
Students can take any courses they wish with the exceptions: 
 1) Undergraduate students cannot take courses at the graduate level (usually numbered 500 or higher)
 2) Graduate students cannot take courses at the undergraduate level (usually numbered less than 500)
 3) Any courses described in Bear Tracks as being closed to exchange students or being restricted to only certain categories or groups of UAlberta students. 
 Exceptions include PLAN courses (which would indeed be open to exchange students)
 4) Courses with non-standard fees
 5) Alternate delivery sections of any UAlberta courses
 6) Courses from the Faculties of Medicine & Dentistry (with the exception of courses in the Departments of Biochemistry, Cell Biology, and Physiology), Nursing, Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, and Rehabilitation Medicine.
 7) Courses at the School of Library and Information Studies, Yukon University, Bamfield Marine Sciences Centre, and in the Faculty of Education's English Language School.
 8) Courses for field schools, work placements, or practica.
 9) BTM 441, BUS 101, BUS 201, BUS 301, FIN 430, FIN 436, FIN 440, MGTSC 312, SEM 310, SEM 441, SEM 470, and SEM 471, independent research, internship, study tour, competition, work experience, Honours (numbered 480) and Special Topic (numbered 488) courses in the Alberta School of Business.
 10) REN R 301, 401-405, 501, 603-605, and 900+ courses in the Department of Renewable Resources.
 11) SOC 399 and SOC 499 in the Department of Sociology.
 12) SPH 603 in the School of Public Health.
universities.restricted_areas:

universities.admission_notes:
The Alberta School of Business also requires that you: i) have completed introductory-level statistics, calculus, microeconomics, and macroeconomics by the time of the application (everything else said about the Alberta School of Business is still correct). Everything said about the Faculty of Law is still correct but they have provided an additional warning which reads, "Please note that students interested in an undergraduate-level exchange to UAlberta's Faculty of Law Juris Doctor program should know that it is an after-degree program. The other students in the program will each have at least a 3-year bachelor's degree. Students without similar academic backgrounds are strongly encouraged to discuss their plans with academic advisors at their home institutions before applying for the exchange and seek permision to, if need be, take fewer than the maximum number of courses allowable per term while on exchange."
The additional and/or higher requirements for our Faculty of Law and Faculty of Kinesiology, Sport, and Recreation( https://www.ualberta.ca/en/admissions-programs/exchange-programs/incoming-exchange-application-guide/admission-requirements/admission-requirements.html)
